data.title: Nano Labs receives $1M in USDT from unregistered equity sale
data.description:
• Nano Labs (NASDAQ:NA) received $1M equivalent in USDT from previously announced private placement of 913,714 Class A ordinary shares at $6.46/share, for aggregate gross proceeds of ~$5.9M.
  • NA has issued additional 154,799 Class A ordinary shares to the investors of this private placement.

data.title: European markets dip after a volatile week
data.description:
Update: The revised reflects market movements of 6.45am ET.

London (UKX) -0.45% to 8,644. 

Germany (DAX:IND) -1.46% to 23,035.

France (CAC:IND) -0.95% to 8,119. France imports and exports remained stable in January.

Slovakia’s economy advanced by 1.8% Y/Y in Q4.

Industrial production in Spain shrank 1% Y/Y in January.

Euro Area GDP expands 1.2% Y/Y, beating estimates.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 0.54% lower to 552.9, rounding off a volatile week due to ongoing uncertainty surrounding US trade policy under the Trump administration, which continued to weigh on investor sentiment. Luxury stocks were among the worst performers.

The European Central Bank made the anticipated interest rate cut, but took a more cautious approach to future rate changes while raising its near-term inflation forecasts.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 2 basis points to 4.26%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 7 basis points to 2.82%.

UK's 10-year yield was down less than 2 basis points to 4.69%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).

data.title: Cinemark announces $200M share buyback program
data.description:
• Cinemark (NYSE:CNK) said its board approved a share repurchase program under which the company can buy back up to $200 million of its outstanding stock.
  • The program will start on March 11 and will continue until the authorized repurchase amount is reached, or the board suspends or terminates the program, whichever occurs first. 
  • Shares +0.93% in premarket trade.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson axes late-stage program for depression therapy
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) has decided to discontinue a Phase 3 program for its depression therapy aticaprant due to insufficient efficacy, the pharma giant announced.

Known as VENTURA, the multi-center program comprised placebo-controlled trials designed to evaluate aticaprant as an adjunctive treatment for major depressive disorder (MDD).

The program was discontinued due to “insufficient efficacy in the target patient population,” J&J (NYSE:JNJ) of New Brunswick, New Jersey, said in a statement late Thursday. There were no new safety signals, and the experimental therapy was well tolerated by patients, the company added.

“Based on the potential for this mechanism, the company will explore future development opportunities for aticaprant in other areas of high unmet need,” J&J (NYSE:JNJ) noted.

Aticaprant belongs to a drug class known as oral opioid kappa receptor antagonists, which also include Neumora Therapeutics’ (NMRA) lead asset, Navacaprant.

The Watertown, Massachusetts-based biotech said last month that navacaprant failed to reach the main goals in patients with MDD in its Phase 3 KOASTAL-1 study.

data.title: Intuitive Machines slides again as investors weigh 'off-nominal' moon landing
data.description:
Shares of Intuitive Machines (NASDAQ:LUNR) fell sharply in premarket trading on Friday after a 20.2% plunge on Thursday.

Early reports indicate that the Houston-based aerospace company's second moon lander, Athena, landed on its side. However, in an update on Friday, the company confirmed the lander is currently "operating" on the surface of the moon.

 Intuitive Machines CEO Steve Altemus referred to the landing as "off-nominal" to suggest that the objectives for the mission will need to be met differently than originally planned. Altemus said the team believes the Athena lander is in the general vicinity of the intended landing site.

Before the moon landing, Intuitive Machines chief technology officer Tim Crain said Athena's cameras captured "some really, really great pictures flying over the South Pole."

Intuitive Machines (NASDAQ:LUNR) has a general goal to offer transportation and payload delivery services to the lunar surface. This includes deploying scientific instruments, satellites, and other cargo to the Moon. The company is part of NASA's Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) program, which aims to encourage private industry to develop cost-effective robotic spacecraft for lunar exploration.

Shares of Intuitive Machines (LUNR) were down 34.2% in premarket action to $7.42 vs. the 52-week range of $3.15 to $24.94. Short interest on LUNR stands at 19.0% of the total float.

data.title: inTEST renews $10 million stock buyback plan
data.description:
• inTEST (NYSE:INTT) said its board has renewed its $10 million stock repurchase plan, which has ~$9 million remaining.
  • Initially, the board had authorized the repurchase of its common stock through open market purchases starting November 17 and continuing through November 17, 2024.
  • Since the start of the plan and till its initial expiration date, the company has repurchased 141.1K shares for $1.04M. The balance of the plan, or $8.96M, is available for additional repurchases.

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Gold set for weekly gain amid geopolitical tensions, crude oil dips
data.description:
Crude oil (CL1:COM) prices climbed 1.7% to $67.48 per barrel, supported by easing U.S. tariff concerns and expectations of further economic stimulus from China. 

Brent (CO1:COM) prices gained ~1.8% to $70.72 per barrel amid tariff uncertainties. Following OPEC's plans to boost oil output in April, Goldman Sachs cited downside risks to its average Brent projections for 2025 and 2026, including lower demand based on recent U.S. activity data and tariff escalation. The bank had forecast Brent oil to average $78/$73 and U.S. West Texas Intermediate oil to average around $74/$68 per barrel for 2025/2026.

Natural gas (NG1:COM) dipped ~2%, pressured by near-record output and expectations of lower demand next week due to mild weather through late March. Record flows to LNG export facilities and worries about possible declines in Canadian gas shipments following the recently imposed U.S. tariffs on Canada and Mexico, however, restricted the downside.

Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) prices gained about 0.34% to $2920.15, on track for weekly gains as the U.S. dollar lingered near a four-month low and trade policy uncertainty. The yellow precious metal is set for a weekly gain of nearly 2%. Other precious metals were subdued. Platinum traded 0.69% lower at 968.1 an ounce, while silver (XAGUSD:CUR) fell 0.3% to $32.56 an ounce.

Copper (HG1:COM) prices fell 0.66% on Friday after hitting a three-week high in the previous session.

ETFs to track: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (NYSEARCA:GDXJ), (NYSEARCA:IAU), (NYSEARCA:NUGT), (NYSEARCA:PHYS), (NYSEARCA:GLDM), (BATS:AAAU), (NYSEARCA:SGOL), (NASDAQ:RING), (NYSEARCA:BAR), (NYSEARCA:OUNZ), (NYSEARCA:SLV), (NYSEARCA:PSLV), (NYSEARCA:SIVR), (NYSEARCA:SIL), (NYSEARCA:SILJ)

data.title: Stock index futures higher as Broadcom issues upbeat outlook, eyes on jobs report
data.description:
Stock index futures climbed on Friday as Broadcom (AVGO) results brought cheer to the traders, while investors looked ahead to the key labor data.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.5%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.27%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.97%.

U.S. stocks were slammed lower Thursday, with the S&P 500 marking its lowest close since September, as pressure from tech stocks, labor market weakness, and tariff-policy uncertainty took their toll on large-cap equities.

“Fierce cold winds were again blowing in U.S. equity markets yesterday, with ongoing trade policy volatility sending the S&P 500 to its worst day of 2025 for a second time this week,” Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

"Having imposed aggressive taxes on US consumers at the start of the week, US President Trump partially retreated from taxing Mexican and Canadian imports yesterday (assuming goods are compliant with the revised NAFTA). The taxes are delayed until 2 April, but markets will likely price in more retreats," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Broadcom (AVGO) shares rose 12.7% in premarket trade after the semiconductor and software giant reported first-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations and suggested artificial intelligence spending has not slowed down.

Traders will keep on the key jobs data that will land before the opening bell today. The nonfarm payrolls report is expected to show that 160K jobs were added to the U.S. economy in February, according to the consensus of economists, up modestly from 143K jobs created in January. 

Looking forward to today, the main event is the U.S. jobs report, which should get even greater attention given the recent softening in U.S. data. Our U.S. economists expect payrolls to gain +160K in February, up from 143K in January, Sidorov added.

"Today’s U.S. employment report will not really reflect government job losses because the government is not a significant employer and because timing will push the losses into later months. The main future employment hit is likely to be from private sector businesses that depend (directly or indirectly) on government employment," Donovan added. 

Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell is also set to speak later in the day today.
